title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there is $c_{H}>0$ such that any $n$-vertex $H$-free graph $G$ has $\hom(G)\geq n^{c_{H}}$.
context_text: Stated in 1977, this is among the most prominent open problems in extremal and structural combinatorics, asserting that $H$-free graphs contain polynomial-sized homogeneous sets. Erdős and Hajnal proved the weaker lower bound $e^{c_H\sqrt{\log n}}$; the conjecture in its polynomial form remains open.
paper_contribution: Proves that the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture is equivalent to both the polynomial Rödl conjecture (Conjecture 2) and the polynomial Nikiforov conjecture (Conjecture 3), extending several previous partial equivalence results.
paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures

title: Polynomial Rödl Conjecture
statement_text: For any graph $H$ there exists a $C_{H}>0$ such that for any $0<\varepsilon\leq 1/2$ any $n$-vertex $H$-free graph contains an $\varepsilon$-homogeneous set with at least $\varepsilon^{C_{H}}n$ vertices.
context_text: Conjectured by Fox and Sudakov in 2008 as a quantitative polynomial strengthening of a classical theorem of Rödl (1986). A graph satisfying this conjecture is said to have the polynomial Rödl property, which implies the Erdős-Hajnal property via an application of Turán's theorem.
paper_contribution: Proves that the polynomial Rödl conjecture is equivalent to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture; as a corollary deduces that the family of string graphs satisfies the polynomial Rödl conjecture.
paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures

title: Polynomial Nikiforov Conjecture
statement_text: For any graph $H$ there are constants $C_{H},D_{H}$ such that for any $0<\varepsilon\leq 1/2$, any $n$-vertex graph with at most $\varepsilon^{D_{H}}n^{|H|}$ induced copies of $H$ contains an $\varepsilon$-homogeneous set with at least $\varepsilon^{C_{H}}n$ vertices.
context_text: Conjectured by Fox, Nguyen, Scott and Seymour as a quantitative polynomial strengthening of a theorem of Nikiforov (2006) and of the polynomial Rödl conjecture. A graph satisfying this conjecture is called viral; every graph known to have the Erdős-Hajnal property is also known to be viral.
paper_contribution: Proves that the polynomial Nikiforov conjecture is equivalent to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture, establishing all three conjectures as mutually equivalent.
paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures

title: Conjecture 10
statement_text: For each proper hereditary family $\mathcal{F}$ of graphs there are $C_{\mathcal{F}},d_{\mathcal{F}}>0$ such that if $0<\varepsilon<1/2$, every graph on $n$ vertices which is $\varepsilon^{d_{\mathcal{F}}}$-close to $\mathcal{F}$ contains an $\varepsilon$-homogeneous set of size at least $\varepsilon^{C_{\mathcal{F}}}n$.
context_text: A graph on $n$ vertices is $\alpha$-close to a family $\mathcal{F}$ if one can add or delete at most $\alpha n^2/2$ edges to obtain a graph in $\mathcal{F}$. This conjecture, introduced as a natural extension for hereditary families, defines the polynomial close Rödl property; a family with this property also has the polynomial Rödl property.
paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures
